title: Dryopteris eatonii
intro: Dryopteris eatonii is a species of fern commonly found in the mountainous regions of North America, belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris eatonii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat colds, fever, and coughs.
taxonomy: Dryopteris eatonii is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris, which includes approximately 200 species of ferns. The family Dryopteridaceae is a group of ferns commonly known as wood ferns. The classification of Dryopteris eatonii is further supported by its characteristic fronds and habitats. As a member of the Pteridophyta division, it is a vascular plant that reproduces via spores.

It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help with wound healing and pain relief. In traditional medicine, the plant's leaves and stems are often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures.
